content.answer:

month by month.

content.answer:
Hi Ian


Please refer to answers below.


Thanks

Slide 2:



   -

   Why does active shop and skus keep going down mom?

It is due to ops cleanup we started from Jan (clean up non-performing skus
, freeze no payment account shops etc). 94% is from OF, 6% is from JST, the
ADO impact is very small (~17 ADO).




   -

   In terms of the current UE of -1, how does it compare to how much we
   spend on new cb sellers now? Trying to understand if it is low/high right
   now

To new sellers, Swarm UE is worse than CNCB and local SIP who have 3 months
commission waiver, which means we have higher (enough) investment:

[image: image.png]


Slide 4



   -

   It is not easy to tie this table with the (1), (2), (3) items on slide

I cannot tell which is the most important focus and which is the least

Sorry for confusion:

   -

   Channel (1) in page 3 corresponds to Mass pitching in page 4 Channel (2)
   in page 3 corresponds to ERPs extension and Marketing Pitching in page 4

Channel (3) in page 3 corresponds to SAI pitching in page 4

(4) in page 3 is to improve the onboarding process for all the levels on
page 4.

   -

   ERP expansion and SHP Mkt pitching are High priority, with the highest
   ADO potential ( 33.9K ADO for ERP extension and 24.6K ADO for Mkt pitching)
   and medium ADO/man day in page 3 (11 ADO/man day)
   -

   Mass pitching is quick win, we take it as Medium, with Dec 1.8K ADO
   potential but takes the lowest manpower and highest ADO/man day in page 3
   (43 ADO/man day).
   -

   SAI pitching is Low priority given low ADO potential (Dec 1.7K ADO
   potential) and takes the lowest ADO/man day in page 3  (4.7 ADO/man day)


Slide 13

   -

   I probably need to understand better the priority list. What is P0/P1,
   so i can understand better what i should be more focused on in following up
   with this push

According to priority (High/Medium/Low) above, we put the project priority
as P0/P1/P2

content.question: how do i see UE over time broken down by new sku, old sku and total?

content.answer:
Hi Ian: Please see attached documents page 3.

Summary:
1. Mature sellers: onboarded > 90 days vs Incubation sellers: onboarded <
90 days

2. Mature seller UE improves over months, as we will increase price while
optimize marketing spending gradually

2. Incubation seller UE gradually improve from -2.24 in Jan to -1.35 in
Dec, as

1）More investment to build up role model sellers for marketing 产业带 pitch in
1H26

2)  We will reduce spending month by month even when sellers are in
incubation.

3. In Dec Y26: UE is positive, mature seller UE at 0.62, and  incubation
seller UE at -1.35.

Best Regards
Zhang Yu

content.answer:
Hi Ian

To answer your question

*I thought we were spending -1 on new sellers? Seems like it has increased
to -2.2 - -2.4 now? What changed?*

Sorry for the confusion, swarm new seller UE was kept at -2 to -3 from Nov
to Jan (*-$2.24)*. Instead our program P&L (seller+sls+MP PC2) is -1.x (
*-$1.42* for Jan)


Also to address your question on swarm UE vs MP new sellers*: are we
investing more or less than new MP sellers? and what's our long term plan?*

*Conclusion*: we are investing *2.08* usd/order *more* than CNCB MP mainly
due to


   - a) SLS discount to swarm as swarm order weight is heavier, which we
   agreed to enjoy the heavy rate table
   - b) subsidy in seller P&L to drive new sku growth.

*In the long run by end of year, seller P&L needs to breakeven as a whole*
+ SLS discount will stay for those heavy weight items/shops.


*For details, please refer to table below:*
[image: image.png]

Thanks

content.question: wow, i thought you told me before new seller for MP was -0.7?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Pls find reply to your questions

wow, i thought you told me before new seller for MP was -0.7? now it is
actually positive? we are really spending so much more per order..
not adding the MP seller's own P&L UE. *From Seller UE view*, Swarm is
-2.24usd/order, CNCB MP new sellers is -0.7 usd/order (shared with you last
time).
For reference, to drive business, SCS began with a UE of about -2.4
usd/order at 3.5K orders.
[image: image.png]

True that investment in Swarm new shops remains substantial, but positive
trends are evident:
#1: New shops' UE improved from *-5.7* usd/order in Oct ’25 to *-2.24* in
Jan’26, with ADO rising from 0.6k to 1.7k orders
#2: After three incubation months, UE has recovered to around *-1.2*
usd/order. We are working towards to achieve positive P&L on mature Shops
through seller co-sponsorship and optimised investment by *Apr'26*
#3: As the seller portfolio matures, overall (new+old skus) UE is projected
to *break even* , new shops UE is projected to improved to *-1.35 **by
Dec’26*

Thanks
